name: The Autorickshaw Project
description:
Bengaluru's autorickshaws have always been more than a way to get around. They carry conversations, neighbourhood stories and everyday moments that make the city what it is.

The Autorickshaw Project by Foodstories is a collaboration with a collective of passionate artists from across India, bringing the finest food stories to one of Bengaluru's most iconic symbols. Together, we've transformed autorickshaws into moving canvases, each inspired by the ingredients, flavours and stories that define Foodstories.

For one evening, Foodstories becomes a celebration of food, art and culture. From The City as a Canvas, a panel discussion featuring Malini Malik, Founder of General Items, Artist Seema Surana, and Artist Ankita D'Souza, to ingredient-led experiences, inventive coffee tastings, hot sauce tastings by The Wild Jar, and unexpected discoveries across the store, every experience is designed to spark curiosity and invite you to experience familiar ingredients in entirely new ways.

Come for the art, stay for the conversations, and experience Bengaluru through food, creativity and community.

name: Meet the Maker: Taro Collective
description:
Meet the Maker: Taro Collective | SHOWCASE 02: Viewpoint 👁️

Join us for the latest session of our ‘Meet the Maker’ series, part of SHOWCASE 02: Viewpoint.

This Sunday, we are hosting Taro Collective, a Bangalore and Milan-based furniture brand founded by Federico Fraternale, Marco Grimandi, and Shikha Rentala. Recipients of the prestigious Godrej Design Lab Fellowship 2025, the founders also run a multidisciplinary studio practice spanning interiors, temporary spaces, and brand-led environments. Their distinctive work seamlessly bridges Italian design thinking and engineering with Indian contexts, production, and ways of living.

This conversation will trace the making of Taro Collective through the idea of home, the movement of objects, and the core questions that continue to shape the brand. The founders will dive into their work across different cities and clients, the realities of building a design-led business, their relationship with the wider design community, and the unique way Taro Collective approaches product, process, and place. 🪑🛠️

Come with your questions—the session will conclude with an open Q&A.

Event Details

  • Date: Sunday, 19th July 2026

  • Time: 11:50 PM – 2:00 PM IST (Please arrive 10 minutes early so we can get everyone seated smoothly!)

  • Location: General Items, Ulsoor

How to Book

Spaces are limited. Secure your spot by clicking the Book Now / Get Tickets button above. 🎟️
